<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data.</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="cnt">Counter to count the number of input data.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="cnt" width_description="[3:0]">Counter to count the number of input data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <comment>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</comment>
        <case>
            <when><comment>Reset the counter when the reset signal is active.</comment> cnt == 4'b0000</when>
            <then>
                <assign><comment>Reset the counter when the reset signal is active.</comment> cnt = 4'b0000;</assign>
                <assign><comment>Reset the output valid signal when the reset signal is active.</comment> dout_valid = 1'b0;</assign>
            </then>
            <when><comment>When the input data is valid, start counting.</comment> cnt == 4'b1000</when>
            <then>
                <assign><comment>Reset the counter when the reset signal is active.</comment> cnt = 4'b0000;</assign>
                <assign><comment>Set the output valid signal when the input data is valid.</comment> dout_valid = 1'b1;</assign>
                <assign><comment>Output the parallel data.</comment> dout_parallel = {dout_parallel[6:0], din_serial};</assign>
            </then>
            <otherwise>
                <assign><comment>Increment the counter when the input data is valid.</comment> cnt = cnt + 1;</assign>
                <assign><comment>Reset the output valid signal when the reset signal is active.</comment> dout_valid = 1'b0;</assign>
            </otherwise>
        </case>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal.</input>
        <input id="sign">Signed or unsigned operation.</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVIDEND.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVISOR.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation request.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">Ready to receive the 16-bit output.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">Valid result output.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">Output result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="IDLE">0</param>
        <param id="SUB">1</param>
        <param id="SEL">2</param>
        <param id="SHIFT">3</param>
        <param id="DONE">4</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="res_valid" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.</description>
            <condition>
                <description>Update the res_valid at posedge clk or negedge rst.</description>
                <state id="IDLE"/>
                <state id="DONE"/>
                <assert>
                    <description>At rst, the res_valid should be 0.</description>
                    <condition>
                        <description>rst is high.</description>
                        <true>
                            <description>The res_valid should be 0.</description>
                            <false>
                                <description>The res_valid is not 0.</description>
                            </false>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                </assert>
                <assert>
                    <description>At posedge clk, the res_valid is updated.</description>
                    <condition>
                        <description>At the posedge clk, the res_valid is updated.</description>
                        <true>
                            <description>When cnt is 8, the res_ready is 1.</description>
                            <false>
                                <description>The res_ready is not 1.</description>
                            </false>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                </assert>
                <assert>
                    <description>At posedge clk, the res_valid is not updated.</description>
                    <condition>
                        <description>At the posedge clk, the res_valid is not updated.</description>
                        <true>
                            <description>The res_valid is not 0.</description>
                            <false>
                                <description>The res_valid is 0.</description>
                            </false>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                </assert>
            </condition>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.</description>
        <description>Initializes the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.</description>
        <description>Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.</description>
        <description>Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.</description>
        <description>start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps.</description>
        <description>If the counter cnt reaches 8, the division is complete.</description>
        <description>cnt and start_cnt are cleared.</description>
        <description>Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.</description>
        <description>Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result.</description>
        <description>Computes the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR.</description>
        <description>Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.</description>
        <description>Updates SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.</description>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="width_8to16">
    <description>Converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating the validity of the input data.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating the validity of the output data.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="state_0" value="2'b00">State 0 description.</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="state_1" value="2'b01">State 1 description.</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="state_2" value="2'b10">State 2 description.</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="data_out" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Update data_out register on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of reset.</description>
            <condition>
                <description>Reset condition</description>
                <not>
                    <id>rst_n</id>
                </not>
            </condition>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set data_out to 0 when reset</description>
                <constant value="16'b0"/>
            </assignment>
            <altcondition>
                <description>Valid data input and flag is 1, concatenate data_lock and data_in to form data_out</description>
                <and>
                    <id>valid_in</id>
                    <id>flag</id>
                </and>
            </altcondition>
            <altassignment>
                <description>Concatenate data_lock and data_in to data_out</description>
                <concat>
                    <id>data_lock</id>
                    <id>data_in</id>
                </concat>
            </altassignment>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="data_lock" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Update data_lock register on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of reset.</description>
            <condition>
                <description>Reset condition</description>
                <not>
                    <id>rst_n</id>
                </not>
            </condition>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set data_lock to 0 when reset</description>
                <constant value="8'b0"/>
            </assignment>
            <altcondition>
                <description>Valid data input and flag is 0, update data_lock with data_in</description>
                <and>
                    <id>valid_in</id>
                    <not>
                        <id>flag</id>
                    </not>
                </and>
            </altcondition>
            <altassignment>
                <description>Update data_lock with data_in</description>
                <id>data_in</id>
            </altassignment>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="flag" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Update flag register on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of reset.</description>
            <condition>
                <description>Reset condition</description>
                <not>
                    <id>rst_n</id>
                </not>
            </condition>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set flag to 0 when reset</description>
                <constant value="1'b0"/>
            </assignment>
            <altcondition>
                <description>Valid data input, update flag to 1</description>
                <and>
                    <id>valid_in</id>
                    <not>
                        <id>flag</id>
                    </not>
                </and>
            </altcondition>
            <altassignment>
                <description>Update flag with constant 1</description>
                <constant value="1'b1"/>
            </altassignment>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <description>Initialize state, data_out, data_lock, and flag.</description>
        <state>state_0</state>
        <data_out>16'b0</data_out>
        <data_lock>8'b0</data_lock>
        <flag>1'b0</flag>
    </task>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining EW green time is greater than 10 clocks, force it to be 10 clocks. If the remaining EW green time is less than 10 clocks, do not change the remaining time. The EW and NS lights are paired. When the EW light is green or yellow, the NS light is red. When the EW light is red, the NS light is green. Reset signal (active low). Clock signal. Pedestrian button input. Red output signal. Yellow output signal. Green output signal. Idle state. State 1 red. State 2 yellow. State 3 green. State transition logic.
State Condition Next State
idle always s1_red
s1_red cnt == 3 s3_green
s1_red default s1_red
s2_yellow cnt == 3 s1_red
s2_yellow default s2_yellow
s3_green cnt == 3 s2_yellow
s3_green default s3_green
Counter logic.
Condition Value
!rst_n 10
pass_request && green 10
!green && p_green 60
!yellow && p_yellow 5
!red && p_red 10
default cnt - 1
Output assignment logic.
Signal Assignee
red p_red
yellow p_yellow

Define a traffic light controller that controls the traffic light at an intersection. samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating the end of multiplication. Shift count register, 0 to 16. Update shift count register. if (!rst_n) i <= 0; else if (start && i < 17) i <= i + 1; else if (!start) i <= 0; Update completion flag. if (!rst_n) done_r <= 0; else if (i == 16) done_r <= 1; else if (i == 17) done_r <= 0; Update product register. if (!rst_n) yout_r <= 0; else if (start) { if (i == 0) { areg <= ain; breg <= bin; } else if (i < 17 && areg[i-1]) { yout_r <= yout_r + (breg << (i-1)); } } The module performs multiplication of two unsigned 16-bit numbers (ain and bin) using shift and accumulate operations. On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, the shift count (i) is updated. If reset is active, i is set to 0. If start is high and i is less than 17, i is incremented by 1. If start is low, i is reset to 0. On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated. If reset is active, done_r is set to 0. If i is equal to 16, done_r is set to 1, indicating the end of multiplication. If i is equal to 17, done_r is reset to 0. On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, the multiplicand (areg), multiplier (breg), and product (yout_r) registers are updated. If reset is active, all registers are set to 0. If start is high, the multiplication process begins. If i is 0, the multiplicand and multiplier are stored in areg and breg, respectively. If i is greater than 0 and less than 17, the product is accumulated by adding the shifted multiplier (breg << (i-1)) to yout_r if the corresponding bit in areg (areg[i-1]) is high. The final product is assigned to the output yout.

Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. Set to 8 to represent an 8-bit multiplier. Set to 64 to represent the number of partial products generated in the multiplication process. Register to store the value of mul_en_in for use in the next clock cycle. Register to store the value of mul_a for use in the next clock cycle. Register to store the value of mul_b for use in the next clock cycle. Register to store the value of sum for use in the next clock cycle. Register to store the value of mul_out_reg for use in the next clock cycle. Temporary variable to store the value of partial products before they are added. Partial sum calculation based on the value of partial products. Final product calculation based on the value of partial sums.

Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing.

The module multi_pipe_8bit implements an unsigned 8-bit multiplier that takes two 8-bit inputs, mul_a and mul_b, and produces a 16-bit output, mul_out, representing the product of the two inputs.

The module uses a combination of sequential and combinational logic to perform multiplication in a pipelined manner, allowing for improved performance.

The functionality of the module is as follows:

  1. The module takes in a clock signal (clk) and a reset signal (rst_n) to control the operation of the multiplier.
  2. It also takes in an input enable signal (mul_en_in) that controls when the multiplication operation should be enabled.
  3. The module has two input operands, mul_a and mul_b, representing the multiplicand and multiplier, respectively.
  4. The module produces an output enable signal (mul_en_out) indicating when the multiplication operation is enabled and a 16-bit output (mul_out) representing the product of the two input operands.
  5. The module uses a pipeline architecture to perform multiplication, consisting of multiple stages to generate partial products, calculate partial sums, and obtain the final product.
  6. Specifically, the module uses combinational logic to generate partial products based on the input operands and sequential logic to store the intermediate results of the multiplication operation in registers.
  7. The final product is obtained by adding all the partial sums, and the result is stored in the output wire mul_out.

The module is structured to be synthesized efficiently into hardware, with clear separation of concerns for input control, input registration, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. Width of parallel data input. Count of cycles for serial output. Counter register for serial output. Data register for parallel input. Valid signal register indicating availability of serial output.

Parallel-to-serial conversion is performed by shifting the data register (data) and outputting the most significant bit (MSB) to the serial output (dout) in a sequential manner. The counter register (cnt) keeps track of the number of cycles. When the counter register (cnt) reaches 3, the data register (data) is updated with the parallel input (d) and the counter register (cnt) is reset to 0. The valid signal (valid) is set to 1 when the counter register (cnt) reaches 3 and is set to 0 otherwise.

Reset is active low, so that the module works when rst_n is 1. When rst_n is 0, the module is reset and the registers are initialized to 0.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i represents the current bit position of the divisor. The parameter diff represents the difference between the dividend bits and the divisor.

Divide the input numbers A and B into two 8-bit numbers: a_reg and b_reg.

Compare the higher bits of a_reg and b_reg.

If the higher bits of a_reg are greater than or equal to b_reg, subtract b_reg from the higher bits of a_reg and set the quotient bit to 1.

If the higher bits of a_reg are less than b_reg, keep the higher bits of a_reg and set the quotient bit to 0.

Shift the divisor right by one bit and compare again.

Repeat the process until all bits of A are processed.

Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding. 8-bit input operand A (multiplicand). 8-bit input operand B (multiplier). 16-bit output product. Bit width for inputs A and B. Bit width for output product.

Initialize

<implementation>Initialize product to 0.</implementation>

Iterate through each bit of the multiplier

<implementation>For i from 0 to BIT_WIDTH-1:</implementation>

  1. Check if the current bit of the multiplier is set

    <implementation>if B[i] is 1:</implementation>

    Condition met: Add the shifted multiplicand to the product

    <implementation>product = product + (A << i)</implementation>

    <implementation>end if</implementation>

  2. Logical shift the multiplicand left by one position

    <implementation>A = A << 1</implementation>

samples.gvd:
module fsm
(
    input IN,
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg MATCH
);

reg [2:0] state, next_state;

parameter S0 = 3'b000, S1 = 3'b001, S2 = 3'b010, S3 = 3'b011, S4 = 3'b100, S5 = 3'b101, S6 = 3'b110, S7 = 3'b111;

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        state <= S0;
        MATCH <= 1'b0;
    end
    else begin
        state <= next_state;
        case (state)
            S0: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S1: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S2;
                end
            end
            S2: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S3;
                end
            end
            S3: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S4;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S4: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                    MATCH <= 1'b1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                next_state <= S0;
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="fsm">
    <description>Detect a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the FSM.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State of the FSM when the input is 10.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State of the FSM when the input is 100.</enum>
        <enum id="S4">State of the FSM when the input is 1001.</enum>
        <enum id="S5">State of the FSM when the input is 10011.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="MATCH" type="combinational_logic">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</reg>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="combinational_logic">Next state of the FSM.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <enum_case id="S0">The initial state of the FSM.</enum_case>
        <enum_case id="S1">The state of the FSM when the input is 1.</enum_case>
        <enum_case id="S2">The state of the FSM when the input is 10.</enum_case>
        <enum_case id="S3">The state of the FSM when the input is 100.</enum_case>
        <enum_case id="S4">The state of the FSM when the input is 1001.</enum_case>
        <enum_case id="S5">The state of the FSM when the input is 10011.</enum_case>
        <case>
            <enum_value id="S0">MATCH = 0; next_state = S0; state = S0;</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="S1">MATCH = 0; next_state = S2; state = S1;</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="S2">MATCH = 0; next_state = S3; state = S2;</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="S3">MATCH = 0; next_state = S4; state = S3;</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="S4">MATCH = 0; next_state = S5; state = S4;</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="S5">MATCH = 1; next_state = S0; state = S5;</enum_value>
        </case>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Design a frequency divider that divides the input clock frequency of 100MHz into 50MHz, 10MHz, and 1MHz.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal.</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="CLK_in">Input clock signal.</param>

<module id="alu">
    <description>Implement an ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">ALU control signal.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">A 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">A 1-bit output representing a general flag.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="ADD" value="6'b100000">ADD</enum>
        <enum id="ADDU" value="6'b100001">ADDU</enum>
        <enum id="SUB" value="6'b100010">SUB</enum>
        <enum id="SUBU" value="6'b100011">SUBU</enum>
        <enum id="AND" value="6'b100100">AND</enum>
        <enum id="OR" value="6'b100101">OR</enum>
        <enum id="XOR" value="6'b100110">XOR</enum>
        <enum id="NOR" value="6'b100111">NOR</enum>
        <enum id="SLT" value="6'b101010">SLT</enum>
        <enum id="SLTU" value="6'b101011">SLTU</enum>
        <enum id="SLL" value="6'b000000">SLL</enum>
        <enum id="SRL" value="6'b000010">SRL</enum>
        <enum id="SRA" value="6'b000011">SRA</enum>
        <enum id="SLLV" value="6'b000100">SLLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRLV" value="6'b000110">SRLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRAV" value="6'b000111">SRAV</enum>
        <enum id="LUI" value="6'b001111">LUI</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).</implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="ZERO" description="State ZERO">0</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="ONE" description="State ONE">1</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <reg-value id="state">State of the edge detection.</reg-value>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
    The module uses a finite state machine (FSM) with two states: ZERO and ONE. 
    In the ZERO state, the input signal a is 0, and in the ONE state, the input signal a is 1. 
    The module detects a rising edge when the state changes from ZERO to ONE and a falling edge when the state changes from ONE to ZERO.
    The module has three always blocks: 
    - The first always block is a sequential logic block that updates the state of the FSM on the positive edge of the clock signal clk. 
    - The second always block is a combinational logic block that determines the next state of the FSM based on the current state and the input signal a. 
    - The third always block is a sequential logic block that updates the output signals rise and down on the positive edge of the clock signal clk. 
    The output signal rise is set to 1 when the state changes from ZERO to ONE, and the output signal down is set to 1 when the state changes from ONE to ZERO. 
    Otherwise, both output signals are set to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier. Clock. Active-low reset. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Product. 4 The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding "size" number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
For example, if size = 4 and mul_a = 2, then mul_a_ext = 00000010.
This is done to prepare the input signals for the multiplication operation.
The multiplication operation is done using a generate block.
For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).
This is done using the following code:
for(i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1)
  partial_product[i] = (mul_b[i] == 1'b1) ? mul_a_ext << i : 'd0;
This generates size partial products.
The add of partial products:
The module uses registers to store the intermediate sum values.
On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.
This is done using the following code:
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
  if (!rst_n) begin
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1)
      reg[i] <= 'd0;
  end
  else begin
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1)
      reg[i] <= reg[i] + partial_product[i];
  end
end
This stores the sum of the partial products in the registers.
The final product calculation:
On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of the registers.
This is done using the following code:
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
  if (!rst_n)
    mul_out <= 'd0;
  else
    mul_out <= reg[0] + reg[1] + reg[2] + reg[3];
end
This calculates the final product and stores it in the mul_out.

module calendar
(
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg [5:0] Hours,
    output reg [5:0] Mins,
    output reg [5:0] Secs
);
always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Secs <= 6'b0;
    end else begin
        if (Secs == 6'd59) begin
            Secs <= 6'b0;
        end else begin
            Secs <= Secs + 1;
        end
    end
end
always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Mins <= 6'b0;
    end else begin
        if (Mins == 6'd59 && Secs == 6'd59) begin
            Mins <= 6'b0;
        end else if (Secs == 6'd59) begin
            Mins <= Mins + 1;
        end else begin
            Mins <= Mins;
        end
    end
end
always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Hours <= 6'b0;
    end else begin
        if (Hours == 6'd23 && Mins == 6'd59 && Secs == 6'd59) begin
            Hours <= 6'b0;
        end else if (Mins == 6'd59 && Secs == 6'd59) begin
            Hours <= Hours + 1;
        end else begin
            Hours <= Hours;
        end
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="counter_12">
    <description>Counter from 0 to 11, controlled by valid_count.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low)</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal</input>
        <input id="valid_count">Enable counting</input>
        <output id="out" width_description="[3:0]">Current count</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="STATE_IDLE" value="0x00">Idle state</enum>
        <enum id="STATE_COUNT" value="0x01">Counting state</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Current state of the counter</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Next state of the counter</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <case>
            <when condition="!rst_n">Reset the counter</when>
            <then>
                <assign value="4'b0000" to="out"/>
                <assign value="STATE_IDLE" to="state"/>
            </then>
        </case>
        <case>
            <when condition="valid_count && out == 4'd11">Reset the counter when it reaches the maximum value</when>
            <then>
                <assign value="4'b0000" to="out"/>
                <assign value="STATE_IDLE" to="state"/>
            </then>
        </case>
        <case>
            <when condition="valid_count">Increment the counter</when>
            <then>
                <assign value="out + 4'b0001" to="out"/>
            </then>
        </case>
    </implementation>
</module>

Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null.

When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full. Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations. Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations. Active-low reset signal for both read and write operations. Write increment signal. Triggered when a new data is written. Read increment signal. Triggered when data is read. Write data input. Data to be written into the FIFO. Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations. Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations. Read data output. Data read from the FIFO. The bit width of FIFO, which can be configured. The depth of FIFO, which can be configured.

module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);
    reg state;
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            state <= 1'b0;
            wave <= 5'b0;
        end
        else begin
            case (state)
                1'b0: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd31) begin
                        state <= 1'b1;
                    end
                    else begin
                        wave <= wave + 1;
                    end
                end
                1'b1: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd0) begin
                        state <= 1'b0;
                    end
                    else begin
                        wave <= wave - 1;
                    end
                end
            endcase
        end
    end
endmodule

Implement a module of a 64-bit ripple carry adder, which includes several registers to enable the pipeline stages. Clock input Active low reset signal Enable signal for addition operation 64-bit input operand A 64-bit input operand B 65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb. Output enable signal. Pipeline stage parameter to enable the pipeline stages in the 64-bit ripple carry adder.

For pipeline stages >= 1:

1. Registers are added to the input to enable the pipeline stages.

2. At each pipeline stage, the sum values are calculated by adding the corresponding input operands and carry signals.

3. The output enable signal (o_en) is updated based on the pipeline stages and is synchronized with the clock (clk) and reset (rst_n) signals.

For pipeline stages = 0:

1. The sum is calculated by directly adding the input operands.

2. The output enable signal (o_en) is updated based on the i_en signal and is synchronized with the clock (clk) and reset (rst_n) signals.

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for pulse detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </input>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the pulse detection process.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State indicating the end of a pulse.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="dataout_reg">Store the current state of the pulse detection process.</sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module pulse_detect detects pulses in the input signal data_in. A pulse is defined as a sequence of 1s and 0s, where the 1s represent the high level of the pulse and the 0s represent the low level. The output signal data_out indicates the presence of a pulse. When data_in changes from 0 to 1 to 0, it is considered a pulse. The data_out signal is 1 at the end of the pulse and returns to 0 until the next pulse occurs.
    The module has a state machine with four states: S0, S1, S2, and S3. The state machine transitions between states based on the value of data_in. The state machine resets to state S0 when the reset signal rst_n is asserted. When the state machine is in state S3, the data_out signal is set to 1, indicating the end of a pulse. The state machine transitions to state S1 when data_in is 1, to state S2 when data_in is 0, and back to S0 when data_in is 1 again.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate 4 input data and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value>0</enum-value>
            <enum-value>1</enum-value>
            <enum-value>2</enum-value>
            <enum-value>3</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="accu_state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Accumulator state.</reg>
        <reg id="data_count" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[2:0]">Count of input data.</reg>
        <reg id="sum" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[9:0]">Accumulated sum.</reg>
        <reg id="valid_out" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">Valid output flag.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <case>
            <case-item id="state">
                <case-item-value>0</case-item-value>
                <if condition="'data_count == 3'b100'">
                    <then>
                        <assign id="accu_state" value="1" />
                        <assign id="sum" value="'data_in + sum'"/>
                        <assign id="data_count" value="'data_count + 1'"/>
                        <assign id="valid_out" value="1" />
                    </then>
                    <else>
                        <assign id="accu_state" value="0" />
                        <assign id="sum" value="0" />
                        <assign id="data_count" value="0" />
                        <assign id="valid_out" value="0" />
                    </else>
                </if>
                <else-if condition="'data_count == 3'b011'">
                    <then>
                        <assign id="accu_state" value="2" />
                        <assign id="sum" value="'data_in + sum'"/>
                        <assign id="data_count" value="'data_count + 1'"/>
                        <assign id="valid_out" value="1" />
                    </then>
                </else-if>
                <else-if condition="'data_count == 3'b10'">
                    <then>
                        <assign id="accu_state" value="3" />
                        <assign id="sum" value="'data_in + sum'"/>
                        <assign id="data_count" value="'data_count + 1'"/>
                        <assign id="valid_out" value="1" />
                    </then>
                </else-if>
                <else-if condition="'data_count == 3'b11'">
                    <then>
                        <assign id="accu_state" value="0" />
                        <assign id="sum" value="0" />
                        <assign id="data_count" value="0" />
                        <assign id="valid_out" value="0" />
                    </then>
                </else-if>
            </case-item>
            <case-item-value>1</case-item-value>
            <if condition="'data_count == 3'b100'">
                <then>
                    <assign id="accu_state" value="2" />
                    <assign id="sum" value="'data_in + sum'"/>
                    <assign id="data_count" value="'data_count + 1'"/>
                    <assign id="valid_out" value="1" />
                </then>
                <else>
                    <assign id="accu_state" value="1" />
                    <assign id="sum" value="0" />
                    <assign id="data_count" value="0" />
                    <assign id="valid_out" value="0" />
                </else>
            </if>
            <case-item-value>2</case-item-value>
            <if condition="'data_count == 3'b101'">
                <then>
                    <assign id="accu_state" value="3" />
                    <assign id="sum" value="'data_in + sum'"/>
                    <assign id="data_count" value="'data_count + 1'"/>
                    <assign id="valid_out" value="1" />
                </then>
                <else>
                    <assign id="accu_state" value="2" />
                    <assign id="sum" value="0" />
                    <assign id="data_count" value="0" />
                    <assign id="valid_out" value="0" />
                </else>
            </if>
            <case-item-value>3</case-item-value>
            <if condition="'data_count == 3'b110'">
                <then>
                    <assign id="accu_state" value="0" />
                    <assign id="sum" value="'data_in + sum'"/>
                    <assign id="data_count" value="'data_count + 1'"/>
                    <assign id="valid_out" value="1" />
                </then>
                <else>
                    <assign id="accu_state" value="3" />
                    <assign id="sum" value="0" />
                    <assign id="data_count" value="0" />
                    <assign id="valid_out" value="0" />
                </else>
            </case-item>
        </case>
    </implementation>
</module>

    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] read_addr,
    output [WIDTH-1:0] read_data
);
    reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];
    integer i;
    assign read_data = read_en ? RAM[read_addr] : 0;
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            for (i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin
                RAM[i] <= 0;
            end
        end
        else if (write_en) begin
            RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end
endmodule

Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. Width of the data. Depth of the RAM.

Define a RAM array with a depth of 8 and a width of 6 bits, and initialize all data to 000000.

reg [5:0] RAM [0:8-1];

Trigger the write operation on the positive edge of the clock signal or the negative edge of the reset signal.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the RAM array.

if (!rst_n) RAM[0] <= 6'b000000; RAM[1] <= 6'b000000; RAM[2] <= 6'b000000; RAM[3] <= 6'b000000; RAM[4] <= 6'b000000; RAM[5] <= 6'b000000; RAM[6] <= 6'b000000; RAM[7] <= 6'b000000;

Write data to the RAM array when the write enable signal is 1.

if (write_en) RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;

Trigger the read operation on the positive edge of the clock signal or the negative edge of the reset signal.

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)

Reset the read data.

if (!rst_n) read_data <= 6'b000000;

Read data from the RAM array when the read enable signal is 1.

if (read_en) read_data <= RAM[read_addr];

Clear the read data when the read enable signal is not 1.

else read_data <= 6'b000000;

module right_shifter
(
    input clk,
    input d,
    output reg q
);
initial begin
    q = 8'b0; // Initialize q to 0 using an initial block.
end
always @(posedge clk) begin
    q <= (q >> 1); // Register is shifted to the right by 1 bit on each rising edge of the clock.
    q[7] <= d; // The most significant bit of the register is assigned the value of the input signal.
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="right_shifter">
    <description>Implement a right shifter.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="d">Input signal to be right-shifted.</input>
        <output id="q" width_description="[7:0]">Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="width" value="8">Width of the input and output signals.</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <type>sequential_logic</type>
        <description>Register is shifted to the right by 1 bit on each rising edge of the clock.</description>
        <assign>
            <lhs>q</lhs>
            <rhs>(q >> 1)</rhs>
        </assign>
        <assign>
            <lhs>q[7]</lhs>
            <rhs>d</rhs>
        </assign>
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <description>Initialize the register to 0.</description>
        <sequential>
            <stmt>Set q to 0 using an initial block.</stmt>
            <initial>
                <assign>
                    <lhs>q</lhs>
                    <rhs>8'b0</rhs>
                </assign>
            </initial>
        </sequential>
    </task>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer, data_in will remain constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. Clock signal A. Clock signal B. Active-low reset signal A. Active-low reset signal B. Input data signal. Input enable signal. Output data signal. Idle state. Wait state 1. Wait state 2. Wait state 3. Data register. Enable data register. Enable control register 1. Enable control register 2. State register. The module includes a register, data_reg, to store the input data signal, data_in.
On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (data_reg) is set to 0.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (data_reg) is updated with the input data signal (data_in).
The module includes a register, en_data_reg, to store the input enable signal, data_en.
On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, the register (en_data_reg) is set to 0.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (en_data_reg) is updated with the input enable signal (data_en).
The module includes two registers, en_clap_one and en_clap_two, to control the selection of the input data.
On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the registers.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, both registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are set to 0.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is high, the registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are updated based on the value of en_data_reg.
The output assignment:
On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module assigns the output data value.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the output data (dataout) is set to 0.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg).
If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
